executiveSummary: Iteration 000005 delivers a well-structured skill package for the Zikon logo-generation tool. The SKILL.md definition, CLI implementation, integration tests, and documentation are all present and largely compliant with the PRD. Two gaps remain: (1) index.js exports only skill metadata and lacks a programmatic run(params) function that delegates to node cli/zikon.js, leaving FR-4 only partially satisfied; (2) 'npx skills add zikon' by bare name requires an npm-published package, while the codebase only supports local-path installation — making US-002 AC01 and FR-3 partially compliant.
minorObservations: index.js exports a skill descriptor object (name, description, invocation, definition, parameters) but has no run(params) function that shells out to 'node cli/zikon.js'. FR-4 requires programmatic delegation; currently the delegation is implicit — it relies on the agent reading SKILL.md and executing the CLI directly. This works in Claude Code but is not a programmatic guarantee.

'npx skills add zikon' (bare name) requires the package to be published on npm. README.md and AGENTS.md advertise this command, but the package is not published. FR-3 explicitly allows local-path install ('npx skills add ./path/to/skill') which does work; however, the install UX described in documentation does not match what is actually available without npm publish.
package.json in .agents/skills/zikon/ is missing a 'skills' field (or equivalent entry-point hint) that some versions of @vercel/skill tooling expect to locate the SKILL.md definition file.

The root package.json 'test' script references only tests/test_zikon.js. tests/test_skill_integration.js, tests/test_skill_definition.js, and tests/test_skill_installable.js are not included in the default npm test run.

US-005-AC01 description text appears in the AC01 entry but the detailed sub-items (simulate agent, assert svg_inline, etc.) are listed in AC02 and AC03 rather than as sub-bullets of AC01. The implementation correctly covers all sub-items; this is a PRD authoring inconsistency, not a code gap.
conclusionsAndRecommendations: The iteration is substantially complete: SKILL.md conforms to the Vercel Labs schema with all required parameters and output contract; the CLI correctly returns svg_inline starting with '<svg'; all five documentation targets (README.md, AGENTS.md, PROJECT_CONTEXT.md) are updated; and the integration test suite provides thorough end-to-end coverage. Two targeted improvements are recommended: (1) Add a run(params) function to index.js that spawns 'node cli/zikon.js' with the mapped arguments and returns the parsed JSON, satisfying FR-4's programmatic delegation requirement; (2) Either publish the package to npm under the 'zikon' name or update all documentation to use the local-path form 'npx skills add ./.agents/skills/zikon' to match what is actually installable, resolving the FR-3/US-002 gap. Both changes are low-risk and self-contained.
